turns.student: What brings you in today?
turns.patient: I've had a persistent cough for about five days now. It started out dry but has become really productive with yellowish-green sputum, and it's been bothering me a lot. I've also been feeling really fatigued and have missed some work because of it.

turns.student: Can you describe the main symptom you're experiencing?
turns.patient: The cough is definitely the worst part – it's so annoying and keeps me up at night. It's a productive cough, which means I'm bringing up phlegm, and it's usually yellowish-green in color.

turns.student: When did this start?
turns.patient: It started about 5 days ago. At first, it was just a dry cough, but over the past few days, it's become more productive with phlegm and the color of the sputum has turned yellowish-green.

turns.student: How often does it happen?
turns.patient: It happens all day long, every hour or so. I've been taking OTC dextromethorphan to try to suppress it, but it only gives me some relief for a short time before coming back.

turns.student: How severe is it on a scale of 1 to 10?
turns.patient: It's an 8 or 9 out of 10 in terms of how bad it makes me feel, and I'm getting really frustrated because nothing seems to be working. The dextromethorphan only gives me some temporary relief, but it comes back just as strongly as before.

turns.student: Do you notice any other symptoms when it happens?
turns.patient: Actually, yes. Besides the cough, I've also been experiencing a bit of difficulty breathing and wheezing, especially when I'm exerting myself or lying down. It feels like my airways are constricting a little, making it harder to get a full breath in.

turns.student: Does it get worse with any particular activity?
turns.patient: Yes, it definitely gets worse when I'm teaching my students or exerting myself physically - all that talking and moving around makes my breathing more labored and my coughing fits more frequent. And lying down at night is actually one of the worst times for me because it seems to make the wheezing and difficulty breathing even more pronounced.

turns.student: Do you have any sweating, nausea, or dizziness with it?
turns.patient: I've been experiencing some pretty intense night sweats - I've soaked through my pajamas every single night for the past 4 nights. It's really uncomfortable and adds to my overall frustration with this cough.

turns.student: Does the pain or discomfort spread to any other part of your body?
turns.patient: No, it doesn't seem to spread to any other parts of my body, but the difficulty breathing and wheezing are definitely confined to my chest area. The cough is mainly in my lungs, I'd say.

turns.student: Do you have any swelling in your legs or feet?
turns.patient: I'm not sure about that. To be honest, I haven't really thought about that, and it hasn't been something I've noticed. My main concerns are getting rid of this cough and feeling better overall, so we can focus on what's causing my respiratory issues.

turns.student: What medications are you currently taking?
turns.patient: I'm taking cetirizine 10mg as needed during allergy season for my seasonal allergies. I've also been taking OTC dextromethorphan, a cough suppressant, for the past week with minimal relief from the cough.

turns.student: Do you have any medical conditions like high blood pressure or diabetes?
turns.patient: Honestly, I don't think so. My last physical exam about a year ago was normal, and I haven't noticed any other symptoms besides this persistent cough that would suggest anything like that. I'm just really worried about getting rid of this cough already!

turns.student: Have you ever had your cholesterol checked?
turns.patient: I don't think so? To be honest, I haven't even thought about my cholesterol levels in a while. My main concern is figuring out what's causing this persistent cough and getting some relief from it.

turns.student: Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
turns.patient: I actually have a social glass of wine on weekends, maybe one to two glasses, but I don't consider myself a heavy drinker.

turns.student: How would you describe your diet and exercise habits?
turns.patient: To be honest, my diet and exercise habits haven't really changed recently. I've been trying to stay on top of things since this cough started, but mostly because I'm not feeling up to my usual self. I do try to eat a balanced diet with plenty of fruits and vegetables, and I get some exercise by walking around during recess while teaching, but nothing too strenuous lately.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had heart disease or heart attacks?
turns.patient: My mom has asthma. That's about it, to be honest. I'm not sure if that's relevant to what's going on with me right now, but I thought I'd mention it since you asked about my family history.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had other serious medical conditions?
turns.patient: Actually, besides my mom having asthma, I'm not aware of any other serious medical conditions in my family. My mom's asthma has been pretty well-managed over the years with medication and lifestyle changes, so that's about it.

turns.student: When was the last time you saw a doctor?
turns.patient: My last physical exam was about a year ago, everything was normal. But I didn't really think to mention this earlier, but after the cough started, I did see my primary care physician and she gave me a prescription for antibiotics, which I haven't finished yet because I've been taking OTC dextromethorphan in hopes of getting some relief from the cough.

turns.student: Do you have any allergies to medications?
turns.patient: Yes, I'm allergic to penicillin. It causes a rash every time I take it.

turns.student: Is there anything else you think I should know about your health?
turns.patient: Actually, yes. Now that we're talking about everything, I've been noticing some sharp right-sided chest pain when I cough or breathe deeply. It's a bit worrying, to be honest. That's something new that's come up in the past day or so, and it's making me a bit more anxious about what might be going on with my lungs or respiratory system.
